figures.caption: Fig. 1 Per-region Dice distributions shown as boxplots for Swin UNETR (orange) and nnU-Net (blue). Outliers are indicated by equally colored dots. Both models exhibit a similar mean Dice across regions although nnU-Net shows much less variance across samples. Higher performance is observed for regions 0-7 and 9 and lower performance is observed for the remaining regions 8 and 10-12.

Fig. 2 Visualization of CT scan slices from a patient with PM in multiple anatomical planes. The image is overlaid with the ground truth segmentation mask (left), the nnU-Net predicted mask (middle) and the false negative segmentation mask (right), showing voxels that were present in the ground truth but missed by nnU-Net.

Table 1 Per-region performance metrics for nnU-Net on rPCI segmentation (mean ± std across patients) using five-fold cross-validation on 62 CT scans. Distribution shows percentage of foreground voxels per region in the dataset. Dice (unitless), HD95 and ASD (mm). Region indices correspond to rPCI regions R0–R12.

Table 2 Interobserver agreement and model performance comparison for rPCI region segmentation. Human metrics (DiceH , HD95H , ASDH) show agreement between observers on five CT scans with triple annotations (mean ± std). Model metrics (DiceM , HD95M , ASDM ) show nnU-Net performance against interobserver consensus (mean ± std). Dice (unitless), HD95 and ASD (mm).
